data.reasoningText:
The user wants to run only the Integration tests. There are 2 integration tests:
1. `Checkout_ValidCart_CreatesOrder` 
2. `ReserveStock_Concurrent_HandlesRaceCondition`

The command to filter by trait in dotnet test is:

```
dotnet test --filter "Category=Integration"
```

Let me run it.

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-337e0e6f9fcf46259be44770a710fe45 && dotnet test --filter \"Category=Integration\" 2>&1", "description": "Run only Integration trait tests", "initial_wait": 30}

data.content: The filter didn't take effect — 5 tests ran instead of the expected 2. This is because the project uses `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport=true` (Microsoft Testing Platform). The filter syntax is different. Let me try the correct approach.
data.reasoningText:
The output shows "total: 5" but there should only be 2 integration tests. It seems the filter didn't work as expected. This is likely because the project uses `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport=true` and xUnit v3, which uses the Microsoft Testing Platform (MTP). With MTP, filtering works differently.

For MTP, the filter syntax uses `--filter` but may require a different expression format. Let me check the xUnit v3 / MTP documentation or try different filter syntax.

With the Microsoft Testing Platform, the filter uses a different syntax. Let me try using the `--treenode-filter` option or the MTP filter syntax.


I need to reconsider how the filter is being passed through the wrapper. With `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport=true`, `dotnet test` acts as a wrapper around the testing platform, so the filter syntax might need adjustment—trying variations like `--filter "trait=Category=Integration"`, `/TestCategory=Integration`, or `--filter "Category=Integration"` to see which one actually works with xUnit v3 and MTP. The filter definitely isn't working since there are only 2 integration tests in the source but 5 are running. Let me explore different filter syntax options to see what the test platform actually accepts.

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-337e0e6f9fcf46259be44770a710fe45 && dotnet test -- --treenode-filter \"/**/[Category=Integration]/**\" 2>&1", "description": "Run with MTP treenode filter", "initial_wait": 30}

Unknown option '--treenode-filter'
Usage dotnet exec TestProject.dll [option providers] [extension option providers]

Execute a .NET Test Application.
--
    --filter-class
        Run all methods in a given test class. Pass one or more fully qualified type names (i.e.,
        'MyNamespace.MyClass' or 'MyNamespace.MyClass+InnerClass'). Wildcard '*' is supported at
        the beginning and/or end of each filter.
            Note: Specifying more than one is an OR operation.
                  This is categorized as a simple filter. You cannot use both simple filters and query filters.

    --filter-method
        Run a given test method. Pass one or more fully qualified method names (i.e.,
        'MyNamespace.MyClass.MyTestMethod'). Wildcard '*' is supported at the beginning and/or end
        of each filter.
            Note: Specifying more than one is an OR operation.
                  This is categorized as a simple filter. You cannot use both simple filters and query filters.

    --filter-namespace
        Run all methods in the given namespace. Pass one or more namespaces (i.e., 'MyNamespace' or
        'MyNamespace.MySubNamespace'). Wildcard '*' is supported at the beginning and/or end of
        each filter.
            Note: Specifying more than one is an OR operation.
                  This is categorized as a simple filter. You cannot use both simple filters and query filters.

    --filter-not-class
        Do not run any methods in the given test class. Pass one or more fully qualified type names
        (i.e., 'MyNamspace.MyClass', or 'MyNamspace.MyClass+InnerClass'). Wildcard '*' is supported at
        the beginning and/or end of each filter.
<exited with exit code 0>

data.content:
**2 tests ran, 2 passed** — exactly the two `Category=Integration` tests.

Since this project uses **xUnit v3 with the Microsoft Testing Platform** (`TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport=true`), the standard `--filter` flag is ignored. Instead, pass the xUnit v3 runner option after `--`:

```
dotnet test -- --filter-trait "Category=Integration"
```
